what_it_does: CSS module that defines the MorphStage 'reform' motion system for layered UI surfaces. It provides three layout variants (full-viewport screen, flex-fill stack, and flow overlay) and state classes that animate opacity, transform, and blur between entering, settled, holding, and exiting layers.
observations: Three morph-stage layout variants: .morph-stage--screen (fixed viewport), .morph-stage--fill (absolute inside flex region), and .morph-stage--flow (incoming drives layout, outgoing overlays).
Incoming layer is kept at opacity:1 in both enter and in states; only transform and blur animate.
Outgoing layer owns the opacity fade and is described as being retired by a setTimeout.
Uses will-change, backface-visibility, and filter blur for GPU-composited motion.
Includes a prefers-reduced-motion media query that disables transitions and filters.
Comments explain the rationale, including browser background-tab freezing behavior.
standouts: Incoming layer never gated on opacity to avoid getting stuck invisible when requestAnimationFrame/CSS transitions freeze in a backgrounded tab.
Outgoing ghost layer owns the opacity fade and is retired by setTimeout, which still fires when the tab is hidden.
Three distinct layout variants with different positioning strategies.
Explicit 'reform, don't teleport' motion language with asymmetric enter/exit timing.
conventions: BEM-style class naming with block--modifier and block__element-like layer modifiers.
CSS custom property --ease-out used for easing.
Comments document design intent and edge-case behavior.
Reduced-motion accessibility handled via media query.
will-change and backface-visibility used for compositor promotion.
concerns: The setTimeout retirement logic is mentioned in comments but not present in this file, so correctness depends on external JS.
Hardcoded z-index values (3) may conflict with other stacking contexts in larger systems.
Fixed/absolute inset:0 layers assume the parent provides bounds and overflow handling.
No CSS variables for durations or distances; most timing/spacing values are hardcoded.
notes: A CSS motion substrate for layered view transitions that prioritizes staying visible during background-tab freezes and includes a reduced-motion fallback.
questions: What is the exact value of the --ease-out custom property?
Which JavaScript controller adds/removes the enter/in/hold/out classes and manages the setTimeout retirement?
How are layers created, destroyed, and reconciled with React/Vue/other component lifecycles?
Is the blur filter acceptable for users with vestibular or visual sensitivities even under reduced-motion preferences?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it defines a 'reform' motion language with layered surfaces and explicitly addresses background-tab resilience.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a perceptually continuous, robust transition system where one surface appears to reform into another, while avoiding blank or stuck states when the browser deprioritizes animations.
novelty: The deliberate choice to keep the incoming layer fully opaque so a frozen transition never leaves the app blank, paired with retiring the outgoing ghost via setTimeout.
contribution: A reusable CSS vocabulary for layered view transitions that encodes a specific motion philosophy, layout variants, state classes, and a resilience strategy for background-tab animation freezing.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS module defining the MorphStage 'reform' transition system for layered surfaces across viewport, fill, and flow layouts, using opacity/transform/filter state classes and a background-tab-safe incoming-layer strategy.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a performant, accessible, and resilient motion language for replacing one UI surface with another without teleporting or getting stuck invisible.
